ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Nima pu na, puu Uwumbɔr aanimbiin ni ke saan ŋmann mi, bee maabim, bee n‑yaabitiib. M si aachaŋ ni pam, aa mu puu ke aa ga li si nchaŋ ni, ni kitiŋ kimina aanib pu.”
translated: My father, you have given to me, Uwumbɔr has taken my burdens, my father, he spoke to me. I will thank him, for when he gives to me, he is the one who created me.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Le Josef bui ke, “Tidaŋ ngbaan aatataa le ye ke ibon itaa na ye iwiin itaa mu la.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek

translated: And he went to the king of Salem, who was Melchizedek, the priest of God Most High. He brought bread and wine, and he blessed Abram.

translated: The people do not know the path of life, and they do not understand the development that blocks the future. The people lack the strength.

translated: So, the Lord of the earth and the sky above, who created the world and all that is in it, and the one who gives life to everything, and who sustains all things by his word. And he who holds all things together in himself, and through whom all things were made.

translated: Jacob went to take his children to marry Rachel, but when he saw Rachel's younger sister Leah there, he took her to marry without knowing.

translated: When he did the work of the elders, he made a mistake, he took the child's portion, and he did not give it to the child, and he did not say anything, and he did not do anything, and he did not give the share to the owner, and he did not do anything.

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: M ga cha aa maa ki pɔr pam, le saamaal ga ŋa linibokpaan. M ga ŋa tinyoor ŋa aa pu, le aa ga li kpa liyimbil, le aa pu, binib ga kan tinyoor m chee.

translated: And when Leah saw that she herself had stopped bearing children, she took her maidservant and gave her to Jacob as a wife, and she bore him a child; but Rachel said, "Now my husband will honor me," and he went in to her and she conceived and bore a beautiful son.

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Le u nan yin nnyunbun ngbaan ke, “Tipuur aanyunbun.” Nima chee le Beerseba aatiŋ kan kaayimbil. Esɔ aapuutiib aabɔr
translated: In the place of the well of the name, he said, "This is my father's well." Then Nima went to Beersheba and dug a well. Esɔ made a covenant.

translated: After these things, the word of the Lord came to Abram. He said to him, “I will give this land to your seed, from the river of Egypt to the great river Euphrates.”

text: le ki ŋa tinyoor ŋa bi pu, le ki bui ke, “Maa, ki pɔr, aan ki gbiin kitiŋ kee pu, ki li lik kitiŋ ngbaan. Li kpa mpɔɔn ijan, ni inyoon, ni tiwan nimɔk fu ki baar kitiŋ pu na.”

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.

translated: Then he said to him, "Go, Jacob the child is calling you to his father." Jacob replied, "If I go to my father Isaac and he sees me, he will kill me, and if I go to my brother Esau and he sees me, he will also kill me."

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Ni yaa bi cha kan, aa lik ke m ye unibamɔnn aan maa ye. Buyoonn aa ga nan lik i na, aa yaa kan ipiih i kaa kpa mbɔm, bee iŋoob i kaa kpa mpem ni mbɔm na i ponn ni kan, aa bee ke m sun i la.”
translated: "If you do not work, you will not achieve success and you will not prosper. Therefore, they will tell the child to eat bread, and the elder will eat meat along with the food and their share, and they will not understand it."

text: Aa mu yaa kan unii u joo saawaa na kan, ku udaan ngbaan. Ti mɔmɔk sɔ do, timi aanib bee le ga li ye biseeraadam, lik m ni maanib aawan mɔmɔk ponn ni, ki nyan saawan aah ye ni na mɔmɔk.” Jakob aa nan nyi ke upuu Rachel le sun ute Labann aawaa.
translated: Jacob served Laban for seven years in order to marry his daughter Rachel. However, Laban deceived him and gave him Leah instead. Jacob then agreed to serve another seven years, and in return, Laban promised to give him Rachel in marriage after the first seven years were completed.

translated: He who does this work, who can gather all the people together with their children. The one who does the work, he knows the good things, the one who brings the two sides together, he is the one who completely removes the burden.

translated: Uwumbɔr created the children of Sakpen and made them. Uwumbɔr gave them life, wealth, health, strength, peace in the heart, knowledge, wisdom, and love.
